text:
Once upon a time near the ocean, there was a seagull named Gladys. She had a peculiar talent: she could find lost things. One sunny morning, Gladys scoured the beach and discovered a beautiful, intricately painted bird puzzle. It hadnít been there before.

Curious, she flipped it over. It was a piece of art, depicting a mermaid watching waves. That afternoon, she placed it on a park bench. People admired it—until it began to talk.

"Hello there," it said. "Do you recognize me?"

"I—I think you're a piece from an art gallery?" Gladys asked.

"The sea has its own artists. Just call me Minnow."

How ridiculous! Gladys must've thought. But then Minnow winked.

"You've got charming eyes," it remarked. "Just the sort that love adventures."

Gladys grinned. She never imagined finding someone who could talk to birds. That night, she took Minnow back
